 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2aec","title":"P5.1: Secret Detection Scanner","description":"# P5.1: Secret Detection Scanner

**Parent Phase:** Phase 5: Polish & Safety
**Section Reference:** Plan Document Section 14 (Guardrail 3)
**Depends On:** P1.1 (Database Export)

## Goal

Before export, scan all conversation content for potential secrets (API keys, passwords, tokens, private keys) and alert the user.

## Technical Approach

### Secret Patterns (Regex)

```rust
use regex::Regex;
use lazy_static::lazy_static;

lazy_static! {
    static ref SECRET_PATTERNS: Vec<(&'static str, Regex)> = vec![
        // OpenAI API keys
        ("OpenAI API Key", Regex::new(r"sk-[a-zA-Z0-9]{48}").unwrap()),
        
        // GitHub tokens
        ("GitHub PAT", Regex::new(r"ghp_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}").unwrap()),
        ("GitHub OAuth", Regex::new(r"gho_[a-zA-Z0-9]{36}").unwrap()),
        
        // AWS
        ("AWS Access Key", Regex::new(r"AKIA[0-9A-Z]{16}").unwrap()),
        ("AWS Secret", Regex::new(r"(?i)aws[_-]?secret[_-]?access[_-]?key\s*[:=]\s*['"]?[A-Za-z0-9/+=]{40}").unwrap()),
        
        // Generic patterns
        ("API Key", Regex::new(r"(?i)api[_-]?key\s*[:=]\s*['"]?[\w-]{20,}").unwrap()),
        ("Secret", Regex::new(r"(?i)secret\s*[:=]\s*['"]?[\w-]{20,}").unwrap()),
        ("Password", Regex::new(r"(?i)password\s*[:=]\s*['"]?[^\s'\"]{8,}").unwrap()),
        ("Private Key", Regex::new(r"-----BEGIN (RSA |EC |DSA |OPENSSH )?PRIVATE KEY-----").unwrap()),
        
        // Anthropic
        ("Anthropic API Key", Regex::new(r"sk-ant-[a-zA-Z0-9-_]{40,}").unwrap()),
        
        // Generic high-entropy
        ("Bearer Token", Regex::new(r"(?i)bearer\s+[a-zA-Z0-9._-]{20,}").unwrap()),
    ];
}
```

### Entropy-Based Detection

```rust
/// Calculate Shannon entropy of a string
fn shannon_entropy(s: &str) -> f64 {
    let mut freq = [0u32; 256];
    let len = s.len() as f64;
    
    for b in s.bytes() {
        freq[b as usize] += 1;
    }
    
    freq.iter()
        .filter(|&&c| c > 0)
        .map(|&c| {
            let p = c as f64 / len;
            -p * p.log2()
        })
        .sum()
}

/// Detect high-entropy strings (potential secrets)
fn detect_high_entropy_secrets(content: &str) -> Vec<SecretMatch> {
    let mut matches = Vec::new();
    
    // Look for base64-like strings of 20+ chars
    let base64_re = Regex::new(r"[A-Za-z0-9+/=]{20,}").unwrap();
    
    for m in base64_re.find_iter(content) {
        let s = m.as_str();
        let entropy = shannon_entropy(s);
        
        // High entropy (>4.5 bits per char) suggests random/secret data
        if entropy > 4.5 && s.len() >= 20 {
            matches.push(SecretMatch {
                pattern_name: "High-Entropy String".to_string(),
                matched_text: truncate_secret(s, 20),
                line_number: count_newlines(&content[..m.start()]) + 1,
                entropy: Some(entropy),
            });
        }
    }
    
    matches
}
```

### Scanner Implementation

```rust
pub struct SecretMatch {
    pub pattern_name: String,
    pub matched_text: String, // Truncated/masked
    pub line_number: usize,
    pub context: String,      // Surrounding text
    pub conversation_id: i64,
    pub message_idx: i64,
    pub entropy: Option<f64>,
}

pub struct ScanResult {
    pub matches: Vec<SecretMatch>,
    pub conversations_scanned: usize,
    pub messages_scanned: usize,
    pub scan_duration_ms: u64,
}

/// Scan all messages for potential secrets
pub fn scan_for_secrets(db: &Database, filter: &ExportFilter) -> Result<ScanResult> {
    let start = Instant::now();
    let mut all_matches = Vec::new();
    let mut msg_count = 0;
    let mut conv_count = 0;
    
    let conversations = db.query_filtered_conversations(filter)?;
    
    for conv in &conversations {
        conv_count += 1;
        let messages = db.get_conversation_messages(conv.id)?;
        
        for msg in messages {
            msg_count += 1;
            
            // Pattern matching
            for (name, pattern) in SECRET_PATTERNS.iter() {
                for m in pattern.find_iter(&msg.content) {
                    all_matches.push(SecretMatch {
                        pattern_name: name.to_string(),
                        matched_text: truncate_secret(m.as_str(), 20),
                        line_number: count_newlines(&msg.content[..m.start()]) + 1,
                        context: extract_context(&msg.content, m.start(), m.end(), 50),
                        conversation_id: conv.id,
                        message_idx: msg.idx,
                        entropy: None,
                    });
                }
            }
            
            // Entropy detection
            all_matches.extend(detect_high_entropy_secrets(&msg.content));
        }
    }
    
    Ok(ScanResult {
        matches: all_matches,
        conversations_scanned: conv_count,
        messages_scanned: msg_count,
        scan_duration_ms: start.elapsed().as_millis() as u64,
    })
}
```

### User Interface

```
⚠️  POTENTIAL SECRETS DETECTED

Scanned 2,035 conversations, 63,701 messages in 1.2s
Found 3 potential secrets:

  1. [OpenAI API Key] in conversation #1234, message #5
     Context: "...set OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-abc1...redacted..."
     
  2. [Password] in conversation #5678, message #12
     Context: "...password=SuperSec...redacted..."
     
  3. [High-Entropy String] in conversation #9012, message #3
     Context: "...token: eyJhbGciO...redacted..."
     Entropy: 5.2 bits/char

Options:
  [1] Exclude these conversations from export
  [2] Review each match individually
  [3] Redact secrets and continue (replace with [REDACTED])
  [4] Continue anyway (secrets will be encrypted)
  [5] Cancel export
```

## Test Cases

1. OpenAI key detected
2. GitHub PAT detected
3. AWS credentials detected
4. Private key detected
5. High-entropy string detected (entropy > 4.5)
6. False positives handled (UUIDs, hashes)
7. User-provided patterns work
8. Performance: 100K messages < 5 seconds

## Files to Create

- `src/pages/secrets.rs` (new)
- `src/pages/wizard.rs` (integrate scan step)

## Exit Criteria

1. All pattern types detected
2. Entropy detection works
3. User can choose action for each match
4. Scan completes in reasonable time","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-07T05:26:52.518118526Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-07T06:03:12.928872470Z","closed_at":"2026-01-07T06:03:12.928872470Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of coding_agent_session_search-jk3m","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2aec","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-p4w2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:53Z","created_by":"import"}]}
-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2agcj","title":"Port SqliteStorage core CRUD operations to FrankenStorage","description":"TRACK: cass storage migration (Track 3B)
PARENT EPIC: Complete frankensqlite Integration

WHAT: Port the core database operations in SqliteStorage to FrankenStorage using the compat adapter layer.

VERIFIED SCOPE: 13 files have rusqlite imports. Primary target: src/storage/sqlite.rs (~6,864 lines) contains the bulk.

VERIFIED RUSQLITE USAGE (grep counts 2026-02-27):
- query_row with closure: 118 total (sqlite.rs=57, lib.rs=23, plus 10 other files)
- query_map with closure: 64 total (sqlite.rs=14, analytics/query.rs=8, pages/analytics.rs=13)
- params![]: 96 total (sqlite.rs=61 plus 9 other files)
- execute_batch: 115 total (sqlite.rs=95 plus 9 other files)
- .optional(): 9 total (sqlite.rs=8, bookmarks.rs=1)
- .transaction(): 9 total (sqlite.rs=8, pages/export.rs=1)
- params_from_iter: 26 total (sqlite.rs=7, pages/summary.rs=10, plus 4 other files)
- Box<dyn ToSql>: 0 (NOT USED — corrected from prior estimate of 29)

CORE CRUD METHODS TO PORT (in sqlite.rs):
1. insert_conversations_tree() — bulk insert in transaction
2. get_conversation()/get_conversations() — query by ID/workspace/agent/date
3. get_messages()/get_message() — by conversation_id, role, content search
4. get_snippets() — by message_id
5. insert_indexed_file()/get_indexed_files() — index tracking
6. update_indexed_file() — re-index timestamp
7. delete_conversations_for_source() — cascading delete
8. get_conversation_count()/get_message_count() — aggregates
9. search_messages() — text search with LIKE
10. get_workspaces()/get_agents() — distinct lists

MIGRATION APPROACH (with compat adapter in place):
- Replace 'use rusqlite::...' with 'use frankensqlite::compat::...'
- Replace params![v1, v2] → compat params![v1, v2] (same syntax)
- Replace |row| row.get::<_, T>(i) → |row| row.get_typed::<T>(i)
- Replace conn.transaction() → compat transaction()
- Replace conn.execute_batch(sql) → compat execute_batch(sql)
- No Box<dyn ToSql> migration needed (0 occurrences)

 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2b1ebd48","title":"Index Freshness in Robot Meta","description":"# Index Freshness in Robot Meta

## Problem Statement
When searching, agents don't know if results might be incomplete due to stale index. They need this context to decide whether to re-index before trusting results.

## Proposed Solution
Extend `--robot-meta` to include freshness information:
```bash
cass search "error" --json --robot-meta
```

Output:
```json
{
  "_meta": {
    "elapsed_ms": 45,
    "cache_stats": {...},
    "wildcard_fallback": false,
    "index_freshness": {
      "last_indexed_at": "2025-01-15T10:00:00Z",
      "age_seconds": 3600,
      "stale": true,
      "pending_sessions": 5
    }
  },
  "hits": [...]
}
```

## Design Decisions

### When to Include
Only when `--robot-meta` is specified (opt-in to avoid bloat).

### Staleness Warning
If index is stale, consider adding to top-level:
```json
{"_warning": "Index is 1 hour old; 5 sessions pending. Run 'cass index' for fresh results."}
```

### Performance
Freshness check should add <5ms:
- Read last_indexed_at from meta table (already connected)
- Read watch_state.json (fast file read)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `--robot-meta` includes `index_freshness` object
- [ ] `stale` boolean accurately reflects state
- [ ] `pending_sessions` count is accurate
- [ ] Minimal performance impact (<5ms)

## Effort Estimate
Low - 1-2 hours. Builds on existing robot-meta infrastructure.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-11-30T23:54:08.438234185Z","updated_at":"2025-12-15T06:23:15.011862525Z","closed_at":"2025-12-02T02:30:39.350423Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

-{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2j6p6","title":"Port schema migrations V1-V13 to frankensqlite","description":"TRACK: cass storage migration (Track 3B)
PARENT EPIC: Complete frankensqlite Integration

WHAT: Port all 13 schema migrations from rusqlite to frankensqlite using the migration framework.

CURRENT MIGRATIONS (src/storage/sqlite.rs):
- V1: Core tables (conversations, messages, snippets, indexed_files)
- V2: Add workspace column to conversations
- V3: Add source_id, source_kind columns for multi-machine support
- V4: Add bookmarks table
- V5: Add analytics tables (query_log, session_stats)
- V6: Add encryption metadata table
- V7: Add token_usage columns to messages
- V8: Add model column to messages
- V9: Add thinking_content column to messages
- V10: Add workspace_original column for path mapping
- V11: Add full-text search virtual table (FTS5 with TOKENIZE='trigram')
- V12: Add daemon state table
- V13: Add connector_version column to indexed_files

CRITICAL: SCHEMA VERSIONING TRANSITION (PRAGMA user_version -> _schema_migrations)
Existing users have databases tracked with PRAGMA user_version. The new migration framework (bead 39myk) uses _schema_migrations table. The transition MUST handle:

1. On first frankensqlite open of an EXISTING database (has tables but no _schema_migrations):
   a. Read PRAGMA user_version -> get current_version (e.g., 12)
   b. Create _schema_migrations table
   c. INSERT INTO _schema_migrations (version, applied_at) for versions 1..=current_version
   d. Now the migration framework sees the DB as 'at version 12' and only applies V13+
   e. This is a ONE-TIME transition

2. On first frankensqlite open of a FRESH database (no tables):
   a. _schema_migrations doesn't exist -> MigrationRunner creates it
   b. All V1-V13 migrations applied normally
   c. Standard fresh-DB path

3. EDGE CASE: user_version=0 but tables exist (corrupted state):
   a. Detect by checking if 'conversations' table exists
   b. If tables exist but user_version=0: introspect schema to determine actual version
   c. If no tables: treat as fresh DB

IMPLEMENTATION: Add a transition_from_user_version(conn: &Connection) function that runs BEFORE MigrationRunner.run(). This function:
- Checks if _schema_migrations table exists -> if yes, skip (already transitioned)
- Reads PRAGMA user_version
- If > 0: create _schema_migrations, backfill entries, log transition
- If = 0: check if tables exist (corrupted) or fresh (let MigrationRunner handle)

SQL COMPATIBILITY TO VERIFY:
- CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS <- frankensqlite supports this (verified)
- ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN <- verify support
- CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS <- verify support
- CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE ... USING fts5(...) <- depends on 3vvqa gate result
- INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT <- verify AUTOINCREMENT behavior
- UNIQUE constraints <- verify
- Foreign keys <- supported (V1 limitation: no circular deps, which cass doesn't use)
- ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE (UPSERT) <- verify support

VERIFICATION TEST:
1. Create DB-A with rusqlite + all 13 migrations
2. Create DB-B with frankensqlite + all 13 migrations (fresh)
3. Open DB-A with frankensqlite (transition path) -> verify _schema_migrations backfilled
4. For each table: compare PRAGMA table_info output between DB-A and DB-B
5. For each index: compare PRAGMA index_list output
6. All column names, types, constraints must match exactly
7. Verify: frankensqlite can read and write data in DB-A after transition

FILES TO MODIFY: src/storage/sqlite.rs (FrankenStorage migration registration + transition function)

+{"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2j6p6","title":"Port schema migrations V1-V13 to frankensqlite","description":"TRACK: cass storage migration (Track 3B)
PARENT EPIC: Complete frankensqlite Integration

WHAT: Port all 13 schema migrations from rusqlite to frankensqlite using the migration framework.

CURRENT MIGRATIONS (src/storage/sqlite.rs):
- V1: Core tables (conversations, messages, snippets, indexed_files)
- V2: Add workspace column to conversations
- V3: Add source_id, source_kind columns for multi-machine support
- V4: Add bookmarks table
- V5: Add analytics tables (query_log, session_stats)
- V6: Add encryption metadata table
- V7: Add token_usage columns to messages
- V8: Add model column to messages
- V9: Add thinking_content column to messages
- V10: Add workspace_original column for path mapping
- V11: Add full-text search virtual table (FTS5 with TOKENIZE='trigram')
- V12: Add daemon state table
- V13: Add connector_version column to indexed_files

CRITICAL: SCHEMA VERSIONING TRANSITION (PRAGMA user_version -> _schema_migrations)
Existing users have databases tracked with PRAGMA user_version. The new migration framework (bead 39myk) uses _schema_migrations table. The transition MUST handle:

1. On first frankensqlite open of an EXISTING database (has tables but no _schema_migrations):
   a. Read PRAGMA user_version -> get current_version (e.g., 12)
   b. Create _schema_migrations table
   c. INSERT INTO _schema_migrations (version, applied_at) for versions 1..=current_version
   d. Now the migration framework sees the DB as 'at version 12' and only applies V13+
   e. This is a ONE-TIME transition

2. On first frankensqlite open of a FRESH database (no tables):
   a. _schema_migrations doesn't exist -> MigrationRunner creates it
   b. All V1-V13 migrations applied normally
   c. Standard fresh-DB path

3. EDGE CASE: user_version=0 but tables exist (corrupted state):
   a. Detect by checking if 'conversations' table exists
   b. If tables exist but user_version=0: introspect schema to determine actual version
   c. If no tables: treat as fresh DB

IMPLEMENTATION: Add a transition_from_user_version(conn: &Connection) function that runs BEFORE MigrationRunner.run(). This function:
- Checks if _schema_migrations table exists -> if yes, skip (already transitioned)
- Reads PRAGMA user_version
- If > 0: create _schema_migrations, backfill entries, log transition
- If = 0: check if tables exist (corrupted) or fresh (let MigrationRunner handle)

SQL COMPATIBILITY TO VERIFY:
- CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS <- frankensqlite supports this (verified)
- ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN <- verify support
- CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS <- verify support
- CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE ... USING fts5(...) <- depends on 3vvqa gate result
- INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT <- verify AUTOINCREMENT behavior
- UNIQUE constraints <- verify
- Foreign keys <- supported (V1 limitation: no circular deps, which cass doesn't use)
- ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE (UPSERT) <- verify support

VERIFICATION TEST:
1. Create DB-A with rusqlite + all 13 migrations
2. Create DB-B with frankensqlite + all 13 migrations (fresh)
3. Open DB-A with frankensqlite (transition path) -> verify _schema_migrations backfilled
4. For each table: compare PRAGMA table_info output between DB-A and DB-B
5. For each index: compare PRAGMA index_list output
6. All column names, types, constraints must match exactly
7. Verify: frankensqlite can read and write data in DB-A after transition

FILES TO MODIFY: src/storage/sqlite.rs (FrankenStorage migration registration + transition function)
LOGGING: Log every migration step: version applied, time taken, SQL executed","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-27T05:24:53.808316879Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-01T20:30:31.872142624Z","closed_at":"2026-03-01T20:30:31.872119551Z","close_reason":"Implemented frankensqlite migration path: added MIGRATION_FRESH_SCHEMA combined V13 schema constant, build_cass_migrations() using MigrationRunner, transition_from_meta_version() for meta→_schema_migrations upgrade, schema_version()/sync_meta_schema_version() methods on FrankenStorage, and run_migrations() integration in open(). Uses single combined migration to avoid frankensqlite DROP TABLE autoindex limitation. All 70 sqlite storage tests pass (8 new FrankenStorage + 62 existing SqliteStorage).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2j6p6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-11czq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T05:27:58.883432384Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2j6p6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-39myk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T05:27:59.188579487Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2j6p6","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-3vvqa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-27T05:51:08.473762094Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":497,"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2j6p6","author":"PurpleCedar","text":"Added Gate 3 integration coverage in tests/frankensqlite_compat_gates.rs: transition test for rusqlite->FrankenStorage _schema_migrations bookkeeping and a schema-parity test skeleton (currently #[ignore] due upstream frankensqlite autoindex inconsistency on fresh path). Validation is currently blocked in rch by dependency planner fail-open using stale franken_agent_detection API on worker (missing default_probe_paths_tilde in remote v0.1.0), so full cargo check/clippy could not be completed remotely.","created_at":"2026-03-01T06:15:13Z"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2jb85","title":"Reliability: fail fast on non-zero SSH script exits in install/index runners","description":"Harden SSH command execution reliability by failing fast on non-zero script exits in install and index runners. Current helper behavior can suppress failing statuses and return stdout, which masks real failures and creates confusing downstream behavior.

User impact:
1. False-success flows that fail later with low-quality diagnostics.
2. Harder incident triage for remote install and indexing problems.
3. Wasted retries and operator time due to hidden root causes.

Scope: src/sources/install.rs and src/sources/index.rs.","design":"1. Refactor SSH helper to return structured execution outcome with exit status, stdout, stderr tail, timeout state, and elapsed duration.
2. Treat non-zero exit status as error by default; allow specific call sites to explicitly handle expected non-zero semantics when needed.
3. Standardize error conversion so user-facing failures include command phase and actionable context.
4. Align logging format with security hardening work so diagnostics are consistent across remote operations.
5. Preserve existing successful-path behavior and output parsing for zero-exit commands.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Non-zero SSH exits in install and index paths fail immediately with typed errors that include phase and exit status.
2. Expected polling behavior is explicitly modeled at call sites and no longer depends on implicit helper suppression.
3. No successful-path regression in existing install/index workflows; no feature loss.
4. Comprehensive unit tests cover zero exit, non-zero exit, timeout, ssh transport failure, and stderr-rich error output.
5. Comprehensive integration or E2E tests validate that failing remote scripts abort flow early with clear diagnostics.
6. E2E logs include host, step, attempt number, timeout budget, exit status, and stderr snippet for quick triage.","notes":"Coordinate with host-key hardening design to avoid duplicate helper abstractions. Run full quality gates plus SSH source integration tests with verbose structured logging enabled.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-10T02:25:45.950089454Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T06:33:30.310628988Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T06:33:30.310604653Z","close_reason":"run_ssh_command() in both install.rs and index.rs now fails fast on any non-zero exit code, returning the exit code and stderr in the error. Previously the fallthrough returned stdout even on failures, masking script errors. All 202 sources tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2jb85","depends_on_id":"coding_agent_session_search-2779d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:20:54Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2jpl","title":"Add metrics to e2e_cli_flows.rs","description":"## Priority 2: Add Performance Metrics to e2e_cli_flows.rs

### Current State
tests/e2e_cli_flows.rs uses PhaseTracker but doesn't emit performance metrics.

### Required Changes

1. **Add E2ePerformanceMetrics import:**
```rust
use util::e2e_log::{..., E2ePerformanceMetrics};
```

2. **Capture timing for each CLI command:**
```rust
let start = Instant::now();
let output = Command::new(cass_bin())
    .args(&["search", "authentication", "--robot", "--limit", "10"])
    .output()?;
let duration = start.elapsed();

tracker.metrics("cass_search", &E2ePerformanceMetrics {
    duration_ms: duration.as_millis() as u64,
    items_processed: Some(parse_result_count(&output.stdout)),
    memory_bytes: None,
    throughput_per_sec: None,
});
```

### Suggested Metrics
| Command | Metric Name | What to Capture |
|---------|-------------|-----------------|
| cass search | cass_search | duration, result_count |
| cass index | cass_index | duration, sessions_indexed |
| cass view | cass_view | duration |
| cass stats | cass_stats | duration |
| cass health | cass_health | duration |

### Files to Modify
- tests/e2e_cli_flows.rs

### Testing Requirements (CRITICAL)

1. **Verify metrics in JSONL:**
```bash
E2E_LOG=1 cargo test --test e2e_cli_flows -- --nocapture
cat test-results/e2e/*.jsonl | jq 'select(.event == "metrics" and .test.suite == "e2e_cli_flows")'
```

2. **Verify metrics have required fields:**
```bash
cat test-results/e2e/*.jsonl | jq 'select(.event == "metrics") | {name, duration_ms: .metrics.duration_ms, items: .metrics.items_processed}'
```

3. **Verify metrics align with schema:**
```bash
# All metrics must have: name, metrics.duration_ms
cat test-results/e2e/*.jsonl | jq 'select(.event == "metrics" and (.metrics.duration_ms == null or .name == null)) | "INVALID: \(.)"'
# Should return empty
```

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Key CLI commands have timing metrics
- [ ] items_processed captured for search commands
- [ ] Metrics follow E2ePerformanceMetrics schema
- [ ] All metrics appear in JSONL output
- [ ] Metrics values are reasonable (not 0, not negative)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:20:39.226119058Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T19:52:18.375163289Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T19:52:18.375096114Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added E2ePerformanceMetrics to setup_indexed_env (cass_index with throughput), search_basic (cass_search with hit count), view_command (cass_view), health_command (cass_health), stats_command (cass_stats)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"coding_agent_session_search-2jxn","title":"EPIC: HTML Export Complete Redesign - Message Grouping + Premium Styling","description":"# Context & Problem Statement

The HTML export feature has a FUNDAMENTAL structural flaw: tool calls and tool_results render as SEPARATE message bubbles instead of being consolidated as unobtrusive icons within their parent assistant message. Additionally, the visual styling is plain and doesn't match the premium Terminal Noir design system from the reference webapp.

## User Feedback (Verbatim)
- "ALL OF THOSE elements below the top message SHOULD BE SIMPLY ICONS WITHIN THE TOP MESSAGE IN THE UPPER RIGHT"
- "ZEROTH PERCENTILE DESIGN THAT LOOKS LIKE ABSOLUTE DOGSHIT"
- "Look at /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup... for how components are styled in an ultra slick, rich, attractive way"

## Reference Implementation
The Terminal Noir design system in /dp/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/apps/web/app/globals.css provides:
- Deep space palette with oklch colors
- Glassmorphism with backdrop-filter: blur(12px)
- Colored glow shadows
- Electric cyan primary (oklch 0.75 0.18 195)
- Fluid typography scale
- Responsive breakpoints

## Architecture Problem
Current flow in lib.rs run_export_html():
1. Each message from JSONL parsed into individual Message struct
2. No grouping - tool calls become separate messages
3. Renderer iterates individually, creating separate article elements

Required flow:
1. Parse JSONL messages
2. GROUP messages: assistant msg + following tool calls/results = single group
3. Render groups: tool calls become badges in parent message header

## Files to Modify
- src/lib.rs - Add message grouping logic before export
- src/html_export/renderer.rs - Update to handle grouped messages
- src/html_export/styles.rs - Premium glassmorphism styling

## Success Criteria
- Tool calls render as small icons in parent message header (upper-right)
- Hovering on tool badge shows details in popover
- Rich glassmorphism visual design matching reference
- Strong mobile/desktop differentiation
- Polished light AND dark themes
- All tests pass
- User signs off on visual appearance","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-28T21:55:26.170294412Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-29T00:29:23.900567690Z","closed_at":"2026-01-29T00:29:23.900491618Z","close_reason":"EPIC Complete - All 8 tasks finished: message grouping algorithm, rendering, tests, fixtures, validation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
